mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over recent months, the market prices a lower discount rate and easier liquidity conditions. That supports equity multiples and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, especially when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is declining, or if the sign flips and declining FEDFUNDS is followed by negative returns in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals labor-market deterioration and slower household income growth, which weakens earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS is rising while HOUST remains weak, consumers are spending but housing activity is not confirming broad demand strength. That combination implies fragile growth and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and weak HOUST does not produce below-benchmark 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the sign equally well without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and improving cyclical earnings sensitivity, which supports S&P 500 gains over the next month. The effect is strongest when production is accelerating from a prior trough.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by at least 1.0% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if large INDPRO increases are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal fails entirely during recovery months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in GDPC1 marks broad real activity strength and future earnings support, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when GDP revisions later confirm the initial growth impulse.
failure_condition: Reject if higher GDPC1 growth periods do not precede above-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the relation disappears once revisions are accounted for.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising faster while FEDFUNDS stays below inflation momentum, real policy rates fall and financial conditions ease in real terms. That supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through lower discount-rate pressure.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-over-policy gap trigger is not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the gap has no incremental predictive value beyond CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening labor market, PAYEMS growth with stable or falling UNRATE signals resilient payroll expansion and stronger consumption capacity. That combination should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as growth optimism rises.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises while UNRATE does not increase over the same month
failure_condition: Reject if payroll gains without rising unemployment do not precede positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the effect reverses when labor data are simultaneously strong.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-stagnation rebound, an upswing in INDPRO after prior weakness signals inventory rebuilding and improving corporate revenue momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO turns positive on a 3-month change after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if the post-decline rebound in INDPRO does not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal fails specifically when RSAFS also stabilizes.
